reasoning: The Treasury General Account (TGA) balance on 2025-10-22 was an exceptionally high 905,074 million, whereas the Treasury's published target cash balance for the end of Q4 2025 is $850 billion. Because 905k is near the top of the historical range, mean reversion strongly suggests a decrease in the coming weeks and months. We also anticipate standard first-of-month outflows for social safety net programs to lower the balance by early November. For the near-term (Nov 2), a decrease is highly likely. For the medium (Nov 25) and longer term (Jan 24, 2026), the variance increases, meaning there is more chance of temporary upward shocks, so the probability pulls closer to 0.5 in accordance with the requirement for increasing uncertainty over longer forecast horizons, though it remains below 0.5 due to the 850B target.
